title: New Kids On The Blecch

jokes.text: D'oh! [Screams] [Alarm Wailing]
jokes.setup: Homer crashes his car during opening credits
jokes.punchline: Classic Homer catchphrase with crash sound effects
jokes.explanation: Iconic Homer moment that defines the character, though routine by this point in the series

jokes.text: Jesse Owens humiliates Adolph Hitler by outracing his zeppelin
jokes.setup: Documentary about Olympic history mentions Jesse Owens and Hitler
jokes.punchline: Absurd scenario of Owens literally racing Hitler's zeppelin
jokes.explanation: Clever absurdist take on historical events, good visual comedy concept

jokes.text: Bob Beamon shatters world long jump record by jumping so far he causes vehicle crashes
jokes.punchline: The jump is so impossibly long it causes collateral damage
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist escalation but less memorable than the zeppelin gag

jokes.text: [Gasps] Thirty-eight! That's roughly my age.
jokes.punchline: Homer's shocked reaction to realizing he's that age
jokes.explanation: Standard Homer obliviousness to his own age and physical condition

jokes.text: Oh, please. You get exhausted watching The Twilight Zone marathon.
jokes.setup: Homer declares he'll run the Springfield Marathon
jokes.punchline: Marge's cutting observation about his laziness
jokes.explanation: Sharp, well-structured putdown that perfectly captures their dynamic

jokes.text: [Laughs] I'm a regular Billy Crystal. - You got that right.
jokes.setup: Homer tries to make a joke about his comedy skills
jokes.punchline: Marge's sarcastic agreement (implying he's not funny)
jokes.explanation: Decent marital banter but fairly standard territory for the show

jokes.text: I'll do your job for a day, and you do mine. Then we'll see who has it tougher.
jokes.setup: Homer wants to prove running isn't hard
jokes.punchline: Proposes impossible job swap with stay-at-home mom duties
jokes.explanation: Predictable Homer obliviousness, sets up potential callback

jokes.text: commemorating the time Jebediah Springfield ran across six states to avoid his creditors
jokes.punchline: Springfield's founder was actually running from debt
jokes.explanation: Good historical satire that fits the show's world-building

jokes.text: Anti-chafing nipple tape, check, check, and check. [Squishing Sound]
jokes.punchline: Overly detailed nipple protection with gross sound effect
jokes.explanation: Mild gross-out humor, relies on TMI rather than cleverness

jokes.text: now get outta here before I change my mind
jokes.punchline: Unprofessional, reluctant attitude
jokes.explanation: Mild subversion of official race protocol

jokes.text: [Gasps] I hit the wall! This is so painful.
jokes.punchline: Hits 'the wall' almost immediately after starting
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific joke about Homer's instant exhaustion

jokes.text: Hey, I got my second wind! [Gasps] Another wall! I just- Whoo-hoo! Third wind.
jokes.setup: Homer's rapid cycling through runner's wall and recovery
jokes.punchline: Absurdly fast alternation between exhaustion and energy
jokes.explanation: Well-crafted escalation that plays with runner terminology

jokes.text: Faster, rickshaw driver! Faster! - Ow! - Sir, the whip isn't helping. - Silence! You call yourself Chinese?
jokes.punchline: Using human rickshaw with whip and racist assumptions
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns characterization but relies on dated racial humor

jokes.text: No one can outrun The Flash! [Gasps] Curses! One of my super foes has set a trap for me.
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy running in marathon
jokes.punchline: Treating real-world obstacles as superhero scenario
jokes.explanation: Excellent character-specific delusion that fits perfectly

jokes.text: That's not Grampa. Dad's just dehydrated.
jokes.setup: Someone identifies dehydrated Homer as Grampa
jokes.punchline: Lisa's matter-of-fact correction about appearance similarity
jokes.explanation: Good visual joke about Homer aging when dehydrated

jokes.text: But only one will win the grand prize: A walking tour of Springfield.
jokes.setup: Dramatic sports commentary about the prize
jokes.punchline: Anticlimactic, terrible prize for marathon
jokes.explanation: Good anticlimax joke about Springfield's poor prizes

jokes.text: He's running on sheer pluck, moxie and grit. All of which he'll be tested for after the race.
jokes.punchline: Taking metaphorical qualities literally as testable substances
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly crafted wordplay that treats abstract concepts as literal drugs

jokes.text: Hey, I 'winna' the race! Viva Italia! - I love-a you all. I use up all of my English.
jokes.setup: Bart wins race with fake Italian accent
jokes.punchline: Breaks character by admitting his limited fake Italian
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bart's con artist nature

jokes.text: I got a better idea. Let's have a fun run... on his trachea.
jokes.setup: Angry runners want revenge on cheating Bart
jokes.punchline: Turning running terminology into death threat
jokes.explanation: Clever dark wordplay that escalates the mob violence

jokes.text: Mob, stranger. Mob, stranger. Stranger!
jokes.setup: Bart trying to warn a stranger about the angry mob
jokes.punchline: His frantic escalation from 'mob' to 'stranger' as priorities shift
jokes.explanation: Good panic comedy showing Bart's shifting priorities under stress

jokes.text: Well, thank you for letting me chill in your crib. - And thank you for assuming we're hip.
jokes.punchline: Homer acknowledges they're trying too hard to be cool
jokes.explanation: Self-aware joke about generational disconnect and trying to be cool

jokes.text: Marge, it's not the singing that brings in the Sacagaweas.
jokes.punchline: Homer using dollar coin name instead of 'dollars'
jokes.explanation: Dated reference to dollar coins, shows Homer trying to sound business-savvy

jokes.text: My dream is to be a rock star! - And my dream is to get rid of Bart. - But- How many lives must you ruin?
jokes.punchline: Homer openly admits wanting to get rid of Bart
jokes.explanation: Dark family humor that perfectly captures Homer's parenting style

jokes.text: What up, 'G' Money? - Who's next? Ralph Wiggum? - Whee! I'm a pop sensation.
jokes.setup: Bart skeptical about band members being school friends
jokes.punchline: Ralph immediately appears confirming Bart's suspicion
jokes.explanation: Good setup/payoff with perfect Ralph characterization

jokes.text: Wait. These are just guys from school. Who's next? Ralph Wiggum?
jokes.setup: Bart realizing the 'band members' are just his classmates
jokes.punchline: His prediction of the worst possible addition to prove his point
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that uses Ralph as the ultimate example of unsuitability

jokes.text: You call that a flirt? I'm not melting!
jokes.punchline: Instructor's over-the-top criticism of their flirting
jokes.explanation: Mild satire of boy band coaching, somewhat predictable

jokes.text: Isn't this song a little boastful? - No one told me there was gonna be boasting.
jokes.punchline: Ralph's innocent misunderstanding of what boasting means
jokes.explanation: Good Ralph line showing his childlike interpretation of situations

jokes.text: [Off-key] # Party Posse # - Ew! - Thank you, NASA.
jokes.punchline: Producer sarcastically thanks NASA (for vocal enhancement technology)
jokes.explanation: Clever reference to Auto-Tune technology, very economical joke

jokes.text: Now here's a song that your Principal Skinner doesn't want us to play. - That's not true. This assembly was my idea.
jokes.punchline: Skinner immediately contradicts, showing he organized the event
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of typical rock concert rebellion narrative

jokes.text: I like your brand of inoffensive pop rock. - Screw you, man! We're gonna play it anyway.
jokes.punchline: Bart rebels against praise instead of censorship
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment showing Bart's automatic rebellion even when inappropriate

jokes.text: Orange drink? What? Do you live with your mama? - She lives with me.
jokes.setup: Skinner mocked for serving orange drink and living with mother
jokes.punchline: Technically correct but pathetic clarification
jokes.explanation: Classic Skinner defensiveness about his living situation

jokes.text: Orange drink? What? Do you live with your mama? / She lives with me.
jokes.setup: Willie mocking Skinner's choice to serve orange drink
jokes.punchline: Skinner's defensive but technically accurate response about his mother
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment showing Skinner's defensive pride about his situation

jokes.text: I'm wearing a bathrobe and I'm not even sick.
jokes.punchline: Bart's odd metric for success (wearing bathrobe when healthy)
jokes.explanation: Good child logic about luxury and celebrity lifestyle

jokes.text: Really? You saw our B.F. N? - I can't believe I'm meeting Milhouse. Word!
jokes.punchline: N'Sync member more excited about meeting Milhouse than Bart
jokes.explanation: Mild celebrity reversal joke with period-appropriate boy band reference

jokes.text: Crowd Taser? - Yeah. It's perfect for getting through the fans to your limousine.
jokes.punchline: Casual discussion of tasing fans as normal celebrity practice
jokes.explanation: Dark satire of celebrity entitlement and fan treatment

jokes.text: Dudes, we gotta go. Our clothes are getting a little out-of-date. - To the Bandana Republic!
jokes.punchline: Pun on Banana Republic store name with bandana
jokes.explanation: Mild pun about boy band fashion obsession

jokes.text: It doesn't mean anything. It's like 'rama lama ding dong' or 'give peace a chance.'
jokes.setup: Lisa asks what the backwards lyrics mean
jokes.punchline: Homer comparing nonsense syllables to peace activism
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer logic equating gibberish with meaningful protest songs

jokes.text: None of those girls has had three kids, I can tell you that.
jokes.setup: Lisa notices something weird about music video
jokes.punchline: Marge's practical observation about video dancers vs real mothers
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Marge's realistic perspective on beauty standards

jokes.text: Otto, what are you doing? - I don't know. I just got an urge to join the navy.
jokes.punchline: Inexplicable urge to join navy after hearing song
jokes.explanation: Good setup for subliminal message revelation, Otto perfect character for this

jokes.text: Otto, what are you doing? / I don't know. I just got an urge to join the navy.
jokes.punchline: Immediate evidence that the subliminal messaging works
jokes.explanation: Good immediate payoff showing the subliminal message's effectiveness

jokes.text: You're being brainwashed! - Yeah, probably. 'Yvan eht nioj.'
jokes.setup: Lisa explains Otto is being brainwashed
jokes.punchline: Otto's casual acceptance while repeating subliminal message
jokes.explanation: Perfect Otto response - laid-back acceptance of mind control

jokes.text: Subliminal messages? Do you have any idea how insane that sounds? - Is that a navy tattoo? - A navy tattoo? Do you have any idea how insane that sounds?
jokes.punchline: Exact repetition when confronted with evidence
jokes.explanation: Good repetition joke showing character's nervous deflection

jokes.text: Yeah, that's right. Lieutenant L.T. Smash.
jokes.punchline: His proud revelation that L.T. literally stands for Lieutenant
jokes.explanation: Good reveal that his stage name was hiding in plain sight

jokes.text: [Moans] How could you soil the good name of Star Blitzzz Promotions?
jokes.punchline: Fake concern about obviously fake company name
jokes.explanation: Mild joke about obvious fake business names

jokes.text: Going back to Elvis. Then there was Sgt. Pepper's, the Captain & Tennille, and the Kiss army.
jokes.setup: Explaining history of military recruitment through music
jokes.punchline: Taking military-themed band names literally as recruitment
jokes.explanation: Clever reinterpretation of band names as literal military recruitment

jokes.text: It's a three-pronged attack. Subliminal, liminal, and super liminal. - Super liminal? - I'll show you. Hey, you! Join the navy! - Uh, yeah, all right. - I'm in.
jokes.punchline: Super liminal is just yelling at people, which works
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation joke that perfectly deconstructs persuasion methods, became iconic

jokes.text: Are you sure someone hasn't been bitten by the jealousy bug? [Chuckles] Here comes the jealousy bug! Gonna get ya! Gonna get ya! - Hey! Stop! Cut it out. You're a grown man.
jokes.setup: Homer suggests Lisa is jealous of Bart
jokes.punchline: Homer literally acting out the jealousy bug like a child
jokes.explanation: Standard Homer childishness, relies more on character than wit

jokes.text: Good afternoon, and welcome to the U.S.S. Sea Spanker!
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate sexual innuendo in ship name
jokes.explanation: Juvenile sexual humor, relies on innuendo over cleverness

jokes.text: Ow! Hot, hot, hot!
jokes.punchline: Physical discomfort breaking through his performance
jokes.explanation: Basic physical comedy that works but isn't particularly creative

jokes.text: Groovy, dude. - Burn down the barbershops! - I hate America!
jokes.setup: L.T. having hippie flashback during performance
jokes.punchline: Extreme anti-establishment statements escalating quickly
jokes.explanation: Good escalation but relies on period-specific hippie stereotypes

jokes.text: The hippie fantasy again? / They're getting less frequent, sir.
jokes.setup: Admiral finding L.T. in a confused state
jokes.punchline: Casual discussion of L.T.'s apparent mental health issues
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about military mental health issues treated bureaucratically

jokes.text: You won't say it's crazy when you see next week's issue of Mad magazine.
jokes.setup: Admiral explaining why project is ending
jokes.punchline: Mad magazine parody as serious military concern
jokes.explanation: Good meta-joke about the power of satire magazines

jokes.text: When this satirical bombshell hits the stands tomorrow, your band will have as much recruiting power as a wax apple.
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific comparison to wax apple
jokes.explanation: Good specific comparison, though wax apple reference feels arbitrary

jokes.text: So, what songs did I miss? - Dad, L.T.'s gone crazy!
jokes.setup: Homer returns from bathroom during crisis
jokes.punchline: Complete obliviousness to dramatic events
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer obliviousness timing, good character beat

jokes.text: Not Mad! That's our nation's largest mental illness-themed humor magazine!
jokes.setup: L.T. threatens to blow up Mad magazine
jokes.punchline: Technically accurate but odd description of Mad magazine
jokes.explanation: Clever reframing of Mad magazine through literal interpretation of name

jokes.text: Why don't we call it 'Everybody Hates Raymond?'
jokes.punchline: Parody title suggestion for popular sitcom
jokes.explanation: Period-specific TV parody reference, mild humor

jokes.text: We can't let L.T. Blow up Mad! Tina Brown was just starting to turn it around.
jokes.punchline: Specific reference to editor Tina Brown's changes
jokes.explanation: Very specific media industry reference that dates the episode

jokes.text: We heard what you said. - Yeah! Heard it old school.
jokes.punchline: Adding 'old school' to normal statement for no reason
jokes.explanation: Mild parody of boy band speech patterns

jokes.text: I can't read. - I can't sing without dancing.
jokes.setup: Boys need to learn new song quickly
jokes.punchline: Basic skill deficiencies revealed under pressure
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific limitations that create urgency

jokes.text: Nobody pouts going into a jiggy. - Yeah. That's stupid. - I want to twirl!
jokes.punchline: Kids breaking down into basic wants during crisis
jokes.explanation: Good character breakdown under pressure, Ralph particularly good

jokes.text: Aw, man. We could've been on the cover of Mad. - They called me 'Smelson!' Ha-ha!
jokes.setup: Boys disappointed about missing Mad magazine fame
jokes.punchline: Nelson excited about unflattering nickname
jokes.explanation: Perfect Nelson character moment - enjoying negative attention

jokes.text: Smelson. It's funny 'cause you smell.
jokes.punchline: Bart explaining why the insult works
jokes.explanation: Over-explaining the joke reduces its impact

jokes.text: You know, we've had a lot of fun tonight at the expense of the U.S. Navy. But they're out there every day protecting us from Godzilla. - And don't forget pirates. - And jellyfish.
jokes.setup: Homer's PSA about respecting the Navy
jokes.punchline: Escalating from fictional to real to absurd threats
jokes.explanation: Good escalating absurdity with meta-commentary on the episode

jokes.text: Those whack invertebrates will sting you, old school!
jokes.punchline: Boy band member using slang to describe marine biology
jokes.explanation: Good juxtaposition of slang and scientific terminology

jokes.text: D'oh! I can't believe I'm meeting Milhouse.
jokes.setup: End credits scene with Homer meeting N'Sync
jokes.punchline: Homer echoing earlier N'Sync member's excitement about Milhouse
jokes.explanation: Good callback that rewards attentive viewers

tmdb_overview: A music producer selects Bart, Nelson, Milhouse and Ralph to be members of the next hit boy band, who record subliminal songs about joining the Navy.
